title: Cape Verde at the 2026 World Cup: Roster, list, players, group stage schedule and results
original_text: The “Blue Sharks” of Cape Verde (Cabo Verde) are entering the 2026 FIFA World Cup as one of the tournament’s ultimate fairy-tale stories, making their historic debut after stunningly topping their qualifying group ahead of African powerhouse Cameroon. Managed by Bubista, the tiny Atlantic archipelago nation of just 600,000 people relies on a balanced blend of veteran grit and emerging youth. The squad is anchored by 40-year-old historic goalkeeper Vozinha and 36-year-old captain Ryan Mendes, the nation’s all-time leading goalscorer who remains their emotional and attacking reference point. Alongside these veterans, Villarreal’s commanding center-back Logan Costa provides elite technical composure in defense, while 25-year-old forward Dailon Livramento—who led the team with four goals in qualifying—supplies the electric pace and clinical finishing needed to threaten Group H heavyweights Spain, Uruguay, and Saudi Arabia.
